skill.name: video-outpainting
skill.description: Guides video outpainting with the RunComfy CLI. It helps extend a video canvas, change aspect ratio, and choose between Wan 2-7 Edit-Video and dedicated ComfyUI outpainting workflows.
skill.summary: Extend video frames and change aspect ratios with RunComfy video outpainting workflows.
skill.author: agentspace-so

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many risky patterns, but most high-severity hits are false positives from Markdown text and model names, not cryptographic code. The confirmed risks are legitimate but material: the skill authorizes RunComfy CLI execution, sends user video data to RunComfy services, and documents local token storage. No evidence found of malicious intent, prompt injection against the evaluator, credential exfiltration, or hidden arbitrary command execution.

medium_findings.title: External RunComfy CLI Execution
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE. The skill intentionally guides Bash execution of runcomfy commands and limits the scope to Bash(runcomfy *). This is necessary for the skill, but it can still process user-supplied URLs and prompts through an external CLI. Confidence: 0.86. Confidence reasoning: The command examples and Bash scope are explicit, but no arbitrary shell expansion or non-RunComfy command path is shown.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples and Bash scope are explicit, but no arbitrary shell expansion or non-RunComfy command path is shown.

medium_findings.title: Outbound Video Processing to RunComfy
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE. The workflow sends video inputs and prompts to RunComfy model services and downloads generated outputs. This is expected for the skill, but users must understand that media and prompts leave the local environment. Confidence: 0.9. Confidence reasoning: The skill states that the CLI POSTs to the Model API, polls status, downloads results, and uses RunComfy allowlisted endpoints.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill states that the CLI POSTs to the Model API, polls status, downloads results, and uses RunComfy allowlisted endpoints.

medium_findings.title: Local API Token Storage
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE. The skill documents that runcomfy login writes an API token under a hidden home-directory config path. Mode 0600 reduces exposure, but marketplace users should be warned that credentials are stored locally or provided through RUNCOMFY_TOKEN. Confidence: 0.88. Confidence reasoning: The token path and permissions are explicitly documented, and this is a real credential-handling concern even if it is normal CLI behavior.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The token path and permissions are explicitly documented, and this is a real credential-handling concern even if it is normal CLI behavior.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE for most static external_commands hits. Many detections are inline Markdown code spans, fenced examples, links, or prose, not Ruby backtick execution. The real command execution concern is covered separately as RunComfy CLI usage. Confidence: 0.93. Confidence reasoning: SKILL.md is Markdown documentation, and the cited lines are visibly code examples or formatted prose rather than executable Ruby code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: SKILL.md is Markdown documentation, and the cited lines are visibly code examples or formatted prose rather than executable Ruby code.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Text False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. The lines flagged for weak cryptographic algorithms contain description text, model names, workflow discussion, or exit-code documentation. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or custom cryptographic operations. Confidence: 0.92. Confidence reasoning: Review of the cited lines found no cryptographic API calls or hashing logic, only Markdown content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review of the cited lines found no cryptographic API calls or hashing logic, only Markdown content.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Documentation and Service References
low_findings.description: NEEDS_REVIEW but low severity. The hardcoded URLs point to RunComfy, RunComfy docs, marketplace skill pages, or example user CDN input. They do not show covert exfiltration, but they confirm that external service access is part of normal operation. Confidence: 0.84. Confidence reasoning: The domains and context are visible in documentation links and examples, with no hidden or unrelated endpoint identified.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The domains and context are visible in documentation links and examples, with no hidden or unrelated endpoint identified.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Finding Is a Documentation False Positive
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged line advises avoiding Wan 2-7 for hero delivery and using a ComfyUI workflow; it does not collect host, user, process, or environment data. Confidence: 0.95. Confidence reasoning: The reviewed line is quality guidance for video outpainting, not a command or reconnaissance instruction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed line is quality guidance for video outpainting, not a command or reconnaissance instruction.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash-Scoped External Tool Invocation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uses RunComfy CLI commands through Bash scope. This is a legitimate capability, but marketplace users should treat prompts, URLs, and output paths as untrusted inputs. Confidence: 0.86. Confidence reasoning: Command examples are explicit and scoped to runcomfy, with no evidence of arbitrary command execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Command examples are explicit and scoped to runcomfy, with no evidence of arbitrary command execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote API Submission and Result Download
dangerous_patterns.description: The documented CLI flow posts video-processing requests, polls status, and downloads generated files. This can expose user media to the RunComfy service by design. Confidence: 0.9. Confidence reasoning: The network behavior is described directly in the skill documentation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The network behavior is described directly in the skill documentation.

content.value_statement: Video teams often need to adapt footage to new aspect ratios without cropping important action. This skill guides RunComfy CLI and ComfyUI workflow choices for spatial video outpainting.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to use RunComfy CLI for Wan 2-7 Edit-Video canvas extension.
Provides prompt guidance for changing vertical video to horizontal video.
Helps choose between CLI-based edits and ComfyUI outpainting workflows.
Lists common outpainting routes for social, banner, and archive footage.
Documents relevant RunComfy endpoints, output handling, and exit codes.
limitations: It does not perform video processing without the RunComfy CLI and service access.
Wan 2-7 Edit-Video may produce visible seam or motion artifacts.
Dedicated ComfyUI workflows are described as GUI workflows, not CLI endpoints.
Input videos must be available through user-approved local paths or URLs.

use_cases.title: Adapt short-form clips for YouTube
use_cases.description: Convert vertical clips into wider compositions while preserving the central action.

use_cases.title: Prepare campaign banner footage
use_cases.description: Extend square or narrow video into wide placements for ads and landing pages.

use_cases.title: Evaluate outpainting workflow quality
use_cases.description: Compare fast CLI edits with dedicated ComfyUI workflows for higher seam quality.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Aspect Ratio Change
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a simple format change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use video-outpainting to change this clip from vertical to 16:9 horizontal. Preserve the subject and central action.

prompt_templates.title: Social Clip Expansion
prompt_templates.prompt: Outpaint this Reels clip for YouTube. Add matching background to both sides and keep lighting consistent.

prompt_templates.title: Quality-Focused Workflow Selection
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request that requires choosing a route.
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess whether Wan 2-7 Edit-Video is enough for this hero clip, or whether a ComfyUI outpainting workflow is needed.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Pass Expansion Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for controlled canvas growth.
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a two-pass video outpainting workflow that expands the frame gradually while minimizing seam artifacts and subject drift.

output_examples.input: A vertical product demo needs a 16:9 horizontal version for a website hero area.
output_examples.output: The assistant recommends a Wan 2-7 Edit-Video route, a preservation-focused prompt, and an output review for side-fill artifacts.

output_examples.input: A brand team needs a polished wide banner from square campaign footage.
output_examples.output: The assistant suggests using a dedicated ComfyUI outpainting workflow because seam quality and temporal consistency are important.

output_examples.input: An editor asks whether the skill can add more seconds to a clip.
output_examples.output: The assistant explains that this skill expands spatial canvas only and points to video extension for temporal changes.

best_practices: Use only video URLs or files that the user explicitly approved for processing.
State the target aspect ratio and the areas that should be extended.
Review outputs for seam artifacts, subject drift, and inconsistent lighting.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to process private videos without user consent.
Do not expect prompt-only outpainting to guarantee hero-grade seams.
Do not use it for temporal video extension or mask-based inpainting tasks.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It guides spatial video outpainting with RunComfy, including aspect ratio changes and canvas expansion.

faq.question: Does it create longer videos?
faq.answer: No. It expands the visible frame area, not the duration of the clip.

faq.question: Does it require RunComfy access?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented workflow depends on the RunComfy CLI, service access, and authentication.

faq.question: Can it handle professional ad delivery?
faq.answer: It can guide workflow selection, but strict seam quality may require a dedicated ComfyUI workflow.

faq.question: What inputs should users provide?
faq.answer: Provide an approved video source, target aspect ratio, and clear instructions for how the new areas should look.

faq.question: Is network access involved?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill describes sending video processing requests to RunComfy endpoints through the CLI.
